snippets.title: Basic UMAP 2D embedding with umap-learn
snippets.code:
import umap
import numpy as np
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

X_scaled = StandardScaler().fit_transform(X)

reducer = umap.UMAP(
    n_components=2,
    n_neighbors=30,
    min_dist=0.1,
    metric="euclidean",
    random_state=42,
)

embedding = reducer.fit_transform(X_scaled)
print("embedding shape:", embedding.shape)  # (n_samples, 2)
snippets.explanation: Standardize when feature scales differ; n_neighbors and min_dist are the primary knobs for local/global balance and cluster tightness.

snippets.title: Compare n_neighbors settings on the same data
snippets.code:
import umap

for k in [5, 30, 100]:
    emb = umap.UMAP(
        n_neighbors=k,
        min_dist=0.1,
        random_state=42,
    ).fit_transform(X_scaled)
    print(f"n_neighbors={k:3d}  embedding std:", emb.std(axis=0).round(3))
snippets.explanation: Small k emphasizes local patches; large k pulls in distant neighbors so the fuzzy graph encodes more global connectivity.

snippets.title: Sweep min_dist for cluster tightness
snippets.code:
import umap
import numpy as np
from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist

for d in [0.0, 0.1, 0.5]:
    emb = umap.UMAP(
        n_neighbors=30,
        min_dist=d,
        random_state=42,
    ).fit_transform(X_scaled)
    print(f"min_dist={d}  mean pairwise dist:", pdist(emb).mean().round(3))
snippets.explanation: Lower min_dist allows tighter packing in the embedding; higher values spread points for a more global-looking layout.

snippets.title: PCA baseline then UMAP (link, do not duplicate PCA theory)
snippets.code:
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
import umap

Z_pca = PCA(n_components=2).fit_transform(X_scaled)  # linear baseline
Z_umap = umap.UMAP(n_components=2, random_state=42).fit_transform(X_scaled)

print("PCA shape:", Z_pca.shape, "UMAP shape:", Z_umap.shape)
snippets.explanation: Plot both for comparison: PCA shows variance axes; UMAP targets non-linear neighborhood structure. See PCA and embedding-visualization topics for interpretation.
